summary: AI-assisted decompilation PR that was revised down to 8 claimed objdiff-verified 100% byte-perfect matches across Crazy Hand, Ice Mountain, Pura, and Old Kuri item code. It was closed unmerged because most changes were later covered by other PRs. The PR is most useful as a process lesson: keep AI/m2c-generated submissions tightly scoped to 100% matches, avoid raw pointer arithmetic where struct fields or M2C_FIELD are appropriate, use m2c flags such as --valid-syntax --no-casts, pass union-field hints for GroundVars/FighterVars/ItemVars, and follow project variable naming conventions.
what_changed: Changed Crazy Hand ftCh_Damage2_Anim to call Fighter_ChangeMotionState with ftMh_MS_Damage2 instead of ftMh_MS_Damage, described as a motion state enum fix.

Implemented or revised Ice Mountain functions grIceMt_801F7F1C and grIceMt_801F81B4 as small ground-material loops that inspect data near offset 0xF8 and call grMaterial_801C8CDC when a referenced ground object exists.

Implemented grIceMt_801F929C as a stage animation state-machine routine that updates s16 state fields, calls mpLib_80057BC0 or mpJointListAdd at configured frame thresholds, and clears the active flag when grAnime_801C83D0 completes.

Implemented fn_801F9338 and fn_801F9448 collision callback variants that check a collision-data flag nibble, initialize Ice Mountain animation state, trigger grAnime_801C7A04/grAnime_801C7B24/grAnime_801C78FC sequences, then delegate to grIceMt_801FA7F0.

Implemented grPura_80212290 setup logic: assign a callback at object offset 0x1C, set toon texture image to grPu_803E7620, call lb_80011C18 and grPura_80213250 on the JObj, clear the toon texture image, and start ground animation with the map_id.
Implemented it_802D747C for Old Kuri as a short item state-change sequence calling it_8027CAD8, it_8027C0A8 with 0.0f and 5.0f, it_802756E0, and it_802D848C with state arguments 0 and 2.
Earlier broader AI-generated partial decompilation work was removed from the revised PR; the author stated the final revision contained only functions verified as 100% matches.
smart_moves: Scoped the revised submission to byte-perfect 100% matches only after reviewer feedback, instead of mixing in partial pointer-math-heavy improvements.
Verified matches with objdiff and reported a successful ninja build with matching DOL SHA1.
Ran cargo run -p melee-issues and reported no new issues, with existing issues said to be unrelated.
Fixed trailing whitespace and acknowledged project quality expectations for first-time decomp contributions.

Captured concrete pipeline improvements from review: prefer M2C_FIELD/proper struct field access, recognize existing jobj.h inlines, improve variable naming, and use union-field hints for common module unions.
decomp_lessons: For decomp PRs, especially AI-assisted ones, reviewers prefer small, clean, byte-perfect matches over large batches of partial decompilation with unsafe raw pointer arithmetic.

Raw pointer offset access may achieve a match but is not ideal for maintainability; future work should migrate toward known struct fields, union fields, or M2C_FIELD when the structure is not fully known.

Existing inline functions and assertion patterns should be reused instead of reimplementing equivalent logic; the PR specifically notes jobj.h inlines and HSD_ASSERT string/line-number patterns as something the pipeline should recognize.
GroundVars union typing matters for stage files. The discussion identified GroundVars:icemt as an important m2c union-field hint for gricemt work.
FighterVars and ItemVars are other major union-field families that decomp tooling should infer from file path or context.

In gricemt, offsets around xF8/x100 are structurally ambiguous: review discussion noted grIceMt_801F87FC treating nearby data as HSD_GObj*, while fn_801F9338 treats related data as s16, suggesting unmapped data after grIceMt_GroundVars2 ending near xF4.
Automated code review comments should not be included as part of future PR workflow for this project, per the author's acknowledgement of reviewer feedback.
The PR was closed unmerged not necessarily because the final slice was invalid, but because most changes had been covered by other PRs.
naming_conventions: Prefer common semantic variable names when obvious: gp for Ground*, jobj for HSD_JObj*, fp for Fighter*, and i for loop indices.
Use placeholder names when a variable's purpose is not clear; reviewers preferred that over misleading semantic names.
Avoid leaving decompiler temporaries such as temp_rN where surrounding project conventions provide clear names, though the diff still contained temp_r31/temp_r30 in some generated code.
Function names in this slice remained address-based for many unmatched or newly matched functions, such as grIceMt_801F929C, fn_801F9338, fn_801F9448, grPura_80212290, and it_802D747C.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use objdiff to verify 100% byte-perfect matches before submission.
Use m2c with --valid-syntax to generate M2C_FIELD for offset accesses; reviewer correction clarified that --valid-syntax, not --no-casts alone, is what produces M2C_FIELD.
Combine --valid-syntax --no-casts to get M2C_FIELD output with fewer surrounding casts.
Use m2c --union-field hints for major union families: GroundVars:icemt for ground stage files, and analogous FighterVars and ItemVars hints for fighter and item code.
Path-based union inference was suggested by the author as a pipeline tactic: /gr/ maps to GroundVars, /ft/ maps to FighterVars, and /it/ maps to ItemVars.
For stage collision callbacks, matching may require preserving exact byte/bit flag tests, such as extracting a flag nibble from CollData offset 0x34 and testing for value 1.
PAD_STACK and small dummy stack arrays appeared in the diff as matching aids, but the PR discussion does not provide deeper reviewer approval of those exact tactics.
review_feedback: Reviewer feedback caused removal of all non-100% functions from the PR; the author explicitly stated only 8 byte-perfect matches remained.
Reviewer feedback discouraged raw pointer arithmetic and encouraged M2C_FIELD or proper struct field access for future submissions.
Reviewer feedback noted jobj.h inlines and HSD_ASSERT patterns should be recognized rather than reimplemented by the AI pipeline.
Automated Copilot review was removed and the author agreed not to include automated code reviews in future PRs.
BR- corrected that --valid-syntax is the m2c flag that generates M2C_FIELD; --no-casts only affects casts around the generated access.
BR- recommended using both --valid-syntax and --no-casts for cleaner m2c output.
BR- identified FighterVars and ItemVars as other major union fields in addition to GroundVars.
BR- recommended semantic names such as gp, jobj, fp, and i where obvious, while still preferring placeholder names when meaning is unclear.

BR- highlighted an Ice Mountain struct-layout concern near xF8: grIceMt_801F87FC treats nearby data as HSD_GObj*, fn_801F9338 treats it as s16, and grIceMt_GroundVars2 stops at xF4, implying missing/unmapped data at the end of the icemt2 area.

Copilot review comments in the PR excerpt flagged serious issues in earlier removed WIP code, including possible fixed-allocation overflow in lbcardnew.c, incorrect u32* pointer increments in it_2725.c, null dereference in ifstatus.c, stack out-of-bounds writes in ftmaterial.c, and OSReport varargs mismatches in ftcoll.c. These appear to have been part of an earlier broader revision, not the final four-file slice.
follow_up_queries: Find the PRs that later covered the gricemt, grpura, ftCrazyHand, or itoldkuri changes from PR #2373.
Search for later struct/header work around grIceMt_GroundVars2 offsets xF4, xF8, x100, and icemt.x10E.
Check whether future AI-assisted PRs from this author used --valid-syntax --no-casts and union-field hints.
Look for merged replacements of ftCh_Damage2_Anim using ftMh_MS_Damage2.
Look for merged replacements of grPura_80212290 using grPu_803E7620 and fn_802130D0.
